question_text: A spring obeys Hooke's law up to a force of 50 N. Beyond this force, the spring becomes permanently deformed. A student stretches the spring with a force of 60 N. What will happen to the spring when the force is removed?

options.label: A
options.text: The spring will return to its original length exactly

options.label: B
options.text: The spring will return to its original length because the force has been removed

options.label: C
options.text: The spring will remain permanently stretched because the elastic limit was exceeded

options.label: D
options.text: The spring will break into two pieces

explanation: When the force applied exceeds the elastic limit (50 N), the spring undergoes plastic deformation and cannot return to its original shape. The 60 N force exceeds this limit, so the spring will remain permanently stretched even after the force is removed.
